div.h1: Cache Hierarchy: Latency vs Working Set Size
div.p: The visual signature of cache boundaries
The "step function" pattern reveals cache boundaries:

ul.li: L1→L2: Latency jumps ~3x at 32KB (typical L1 size)
L2→L3: Latency jumps ~3x at 256KB-1MB (typical L2 size)
L3→RAM: Latency jumps ~5x at 8-64MB (typical L3 size) This is how detect_cache_sizes() discovers hardware parameters by measuring latency inflection points.

div.p: ✓ Latency graph shows clear inflection points at cache boundaries ✓ Pointer-chasing technique defeats prefetchers (dependency chain) ✓ L1→L2 step at ~32KB, L2→L3 step at ~256KB-1MB ✓ RAM latency 50-100x higher than L1 ✓ Warmup iterations eliminate page fault noise
